data.content:
Here's my analysis of the legacy patterns and the modernized SDK-style project:

**Legacy patterns identified:**

| # | Legacy Pattern | SDK-style Resolution |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `xmlns` + `ToolsVersion` on `<Project>` | Replaced with `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"` |
| 2 | Explicit `Import` of `Common.props` / `CSharp.targets` | Implicit via SDK attribute |
| 3 | `Configuration`/`Platform` conditional PropertyGroups | SDK defaults handle Debug/Release |
| 4 | `ProjectGuid` | Not needed |
| 5 | `FileAlignment` | Default, unnecessary |
| 6 | `Deterministic` | Default `true` in SDK-style |
| 7 | `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects` | Default `true` for .NET Framework |
| 8 | Explicit `<Compile Include="..."/>` items | SDK uses file globbing automatically |
| 9 | `<Reference Include="System.*"/>` | Implicit for .NET Framework SDK projects |
| 10 | `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` | Use TFM moniker `net472` |
| 11 | `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Auto-generated from project properties |

Now let me apply the modernization:

arguments.old_str:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" Condition="Exists('$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props')" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <Configuration Condition=" '$(Configuration)' == '' ">Debug</Configuration>
    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>
    <ProjectGuid>{12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012}</ProjectGuid>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <FileAlignment>512</FileAlignment>
    <AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>
    <Deterministic>true</Deterministic>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Debug|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
    <DebugType>full</DebugType>
    <Optimize>false</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Debug\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>DEBUG;TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Release|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugType>pdbonly</DebugType>
    <Optimize>true</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Release\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="System" />
    <Reference Include="System.Core" />
    <Reference Include="System.Data" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>
arguments.new_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Remove obsolete AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Remove obsolete AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture. Let me identify the legacy patterns and modernize this to SDK-style format.

Legacy patterns identified:
1. **XML namespace and ToolsVersion** — `xmlns` and `ToolsVersion="15.0"` on the `<Project>` element
2. **Explicit MSBuild imports** — `Microsoft.Common.props` and `Microsoft.CSharp.targets`
3. **Configuration/Platform PropertyGroups** — Debug/Release config groups with default values (SDK defaults handle these)
4. **ProjectGuid** — not needed in SDK-style
5. **FileAlignment** — default, unnecessary


6. **Explicit Compile includes** — SDK-style uses globbing to automatically include all .cs files
7. **System assembly references** — implicit when targeting .NET Framework with SDK-style
8. **AssemblyInfo.cs** — auto-generated from project properties in SDK-style
9. **TargetFrameworkVersion vs TargetFramework** — need to switch to the moniker format like `net472`
10. **AutoGenerateBindingRedirects and Deterministic** — both default to true in SDK-style

Now I'm ready to convert this to the modern format.

data.content:
Done. The project went from **47 lines → 13 lines**. Here's a summary of what changed:

- **Removed**: XML declaration, namespace, ToolsVersion, explicit MSBuild imports, ProjectGuid, FileAlignment, Debug/Release PropertyGroups, explicit `<Compile>` items, system assembly `<Reference>` items
- **Replaced**: `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` → `TargetFramework net472`; `OutputType Library` removed (it's the SDK default)
- **Deleted**: `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` — assembly metadata is now expressed as MSBuild properties (`Description`, `Company`, `Copyright`, `Version`) and auto-generated at build time
